question_text: A student eats a banana that contains 400 kJ of chemical energy. Her body uses some of this energy to run at constant speed on a treadmill. Name two stores to which this chemical energy is transferred during and after exercise.
correct_answer: Any two of: kinetic store (movement), thermal store (body heat/sweating), gravitational potential store (if lifting against gravity). Most answers will include kinetic and thermal stores.
explanation: Chemical energy from food is transferred to kinetic energy (movement) and thermal energy (heat produced by muscles and body). Some may also be stored as gravitational potential if running uphill.
